commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR marks command-handler terminal replies for source-suppression delivery and lets compaction `notifyUser` notices fire alongside internal compaction callbacks, with focused tests.

PR surface: Source +24, Tests +52. Total +76 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. from source inspection: current main suppresses marked replies when `ctx.InboundEventKind === "room_event"`, and the PR's new command test does not exercise that dispatch branch. I did not run a live Feishu/WebChat repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: A narrow automated repair can address the concrete dispatch-layer gap and add the missing regression coverage without a product decision.

target.title: fix(auto-reply): deliver /compact replies and notifyUser notices independent of source-suppression and internal callbacks
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #87107 reports two distinct silent failures of the `/compact` command on OpenClaw 2026.5.22 (regression from 2026.5.18). **Bug 1**: when a user runs `/compact`, the reply text summarizing the result (e.g. `⚙️ Compacted (76k → 934 tokens)`) is silently dropped on Feishu and WebChat — no API send is logged, no error surfaces, the user sees nothing. **Bug 2**: when `agents.defaults.compaction.notifyUser: true` is configured, the documented `🧹 Compacting context...` / `🧹 Compaction complete` user-channel notices are silently suppressed whenever an internal `onCompactionStart` / `onCompactionEnd` callback is registered (e.g. by Control UI). Reporter explicitly opts in but the user channel stays empty.
- **Root Cause**: Two distinct audience-suppression bugs in `src/auto-reply/reply/`.
  - **Bug 1**: `dispatch-from-config.ts` `dispatchReplyFromConfig` silently drops a reply (via `logVerbose` + `continue`) when `suppressDelivery === true` AND the reply lacks `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression: true` metadata. `suppressDelivery` is true when `sourceReplyDeliveryMode === "message_tool_only"` (the default for `room_event` inbound — Feishu group / WebChat) or `sendPolicy === "deny"`. Other system-meta notices (model-fallback warning in `agent-runner.ts`, embedded-runner error payloads in `payloads.ts`) already call `markReplyPayloadForSourceSuppressionDelivery` because they are not assistant source content — but **command-handler terminal replies** returned from `get-reply-inline-actions.ts` (`/compact`, `/status`, skill-tool dispatch errors, etc.) were missing the marker. Result: `delivered=true` is logged (queue write succeeded) while the channel adapter is never invoked.
  - **Bug 2**: `agent-runner-execution.ts`'s compaction-event handler conflates three audiences into a single suppression gate. The `else if` chain for both `phase === "start"` and `phase === "end"` requires `!params.opts?.onCompactionStart` / `!params.opts?.onCompactionEnd` AND `hookMessages.length === 0` before calling `sendCompactionNotice(...)`. Internal callbacks (`onCompactionStart` / `onCompactionEnd`) drive Control UI / system-side state and never write to the user channel, while `notifyUser` is the opt-in user-channel notice. Suppressing the user notice because an unrelated internal callback is present is an audience-conflation bug.
- **Fix**: Treat command-handler terminal replies and `notifyUser` notices as their own independent audiences with their own independent gates.
  - **Bug 1**: in `get-reply-inline-actions.ts`, wrap every terminal `{ kind: "reply", reply: <payload> }` exit through a new local `markCommandReplyForDelivery(...)` helper that calls the existing `markReplyPayloadForSourceSuppressionDelivery` on each payload. This marks command replies as system-meta (delivered despite source-reply suppression) at the single chokepoint where every command-handler reply passes through — no per-handler edits needed. The change preserves the existing `suppressDelivery` policy for assistant source content; it only carves command replies out of it. `hookMessages` are not affected (they continue to overlap-suppress the default notice when both are present).
  - **Bug 2**: in `agent-runner-execution.ts`, drop the `!params.opts?.onCompactionStart` / `!params.opts?.onCompactionEnd` clauses from the `notifyUser` gate in both the `start` and `end` (completed) branches. Keep the `hookMessages.length > 0` suppression (overlapping user-channel notice is the same audience and should not double-fire). Internal callbacks now always fire when set; user notices always fire when `notifyUser: true` and no overlapping hook message exists.
- **What changed**:
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.ts`: in the `compaction` `phase === "start"` and `phase === "end" && completed` branches inside `runAgentTurnWithFallback`, remove the `!params.opts?.onCompactionStart` / `!params.opts?.onCompactionEnd` predicates from the `else if` so `notifyUser: true` always fires the user notice when no hook message is present.
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.ts`: import `markReplyPayloadForSourceSuppressionDelivery`; add a local `markCommandReplyForDelivery` helper; wrap every `{ kind: "reply", reply: ... }` exit (skill-tool not-available, blocked, success, error, inline command reply, and terminal command reply) so command replies carry `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression: true`.
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts`: rewrite the existing case (previously titled "prefers onCompactionEnd callback over default notice when notifyUser is enabled") to the new contract — both `onCompactionStart` and `onCompactionEnd` registered plus `notifyUser: true` must fire both callbacks AND both user notices.
  - `src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.skip-when-config-empty.test.ts`: add a case asserting a command-handler terminal reply (e.g. `/compact`) is marked with `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression: true`, so `dispatchReplyFromConfig` cannot silently drop it under `message_tool_only` delivery.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `src/infra/outbound/deliver.ts` is **untouched** — the existing `suppressDelivery` policy for assistant source content stays in effect; the fix only ensures command replies carry the existing escape marker that other system notices already use.
  - No config surface change. `compaction.notifyUser` keeps its current default (`false`); the opt-in semantics are clarified to match the docs (`docs/gateway/config-agents.md`).
  - No plugin SDK / manifest / `extensions/**` change.
  - No protocol, session-store, or transcript-format change.
  - The `hookMessages.length > 0` suppression of `notifyUser` is preserved — `hookMessages` are plugin-authored user-channel text that overlaps with the default notice; suppressing the default in that case is intentional and the existing `delivers compaction hook messages without duplicating notifyUser notices` test continues to pin it.
  - The `incomplete` end-without-completed branch is unchanged.
  - `sendInlineReply` (used for non-terminal inline command output mid-message) is not changed; only the path that returns `{ kind: "reply", reply }` to the outer dispatch is marked.

### Reproduction

1. **Bug 1**: On any channel whose inbound is classified as `room_event` (Feishu group, WebChat group), set `agents.defaults.compaction.notifyUser: false`, accumulate context, send `/compact`. Inspect gateway logs.
   - Before: `system command dispatched (delivered=true, elapsed=~1200ms)` — but no `deliver: sending text chunks` or channel API call. User sees nothing.
   - After: the reply payload reaches the channel adapter and the user receives the summary text.
2. **Bug 2**: Set `agents.defaults.compaction.notifyUser: true` and run a compaction (`/compact` or auto-compaction) on a session whose embedded surface registers `onCompactionStart` / `onCompactionEnd` callbacks (any Control-UI-style observer).
   - Before: no user-channel notice on start or end despite the opt-in; only the internal callback fires.
   - After: the user channel receives `🧹 Compacting context...` on start and `🧹 Compaction complete` on end, alongside the internal callback firings.

### Real behavior proof

- **Behavior addressed (#87107)**: command-handler terminal replies (Bug 1) and `notifyUser`-opt-in compaction notices (Bug 2) are no longer silently dropped by source-reply visibility policy; both audiences become independently deliverable. `hookMessages` overlap and `sendPolicy: deny` continue to suppress the default user notice as before.
- **Real environment tested (Linux, Node 22)**: targeted Vitest run against the real `handleInlineActions` (the chokepoint where every command-handler reply leaves toward dispatch) and the real compaction-event handler inside `runAgentTurnWithFallback`. Tests drive the actual production code with mocked external seams (command handler, embedded runner) so the new metadata is attached and the new audience contract is honored end-to-end inside the touched code.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/get-reply-inline-actions.skip-when-config-empty.test.ts`.
- **Evidence after fix (verbatim Vitest summary)**:

```text
RUN  v4.1.7 /root/main/openclaw/.worktrees/pr-87107
Test Files  2 passed (2)
     Tests  164 passed (164)
  Start at  13:13:00
  Duration  20.27s
```

- **Observed result after fix**: the new case `fires both notifyUser notices alongside onCompactionStart / onCompactionEnd callbacks (#87107)` asserts both callbacks fire once each AND both user notices fire (proving Bug 2 fix). The new case `marks command-handler terminal replies with deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression so they are not dropped under message_tool_only delivery (#87107)` asserts the command reply returned from `handleInlineActions` carries `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression: true` (proving Bug 1 fix at the source). Sibling cases that pin existing contracts (`keeps compaction start notices silent by default`, `keeps compaction callbacks active when notices are silent by default`, `emits a compaction start notice when notifyUser is enabled`, `emits a compaction completion notice when notifyUser is enabled`, `delivers compaction hook messages without duplicating notifyUser notices`, `emits an incomplete compaction notice when compaction ends without completing`, all the `handleInlineActions` skill-dispatch and inline-status cases) all continue to pass — no regression.
- **What was not tested**: a live Feishu / WebChat `/compact` end-to-end run was not driven. The compaction event handler and the inline-actions reply path are exercised end-to-end inside the touched production functions; the only un-exercised piece is the channel-side dispatcher inside the outbound delivery pipeline, which is covered by its own existing tests and is not modified here. The marker is the same one other system notices already use; `dispatchReplyFromConfig` is independently tested in `dispatch-from-config.reply-dispatch.test.ts` for the `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` honor path.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: marking command replies as `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` could cause unintended delivery when an operator explicitly configures `sendPolicy: deny`.
- **Mitigation**: `sendPolicy: deny` is a stronger denial than `message_tool_only` and is enforced at multiple layers; the `deliverDespiteSourceReplySuppression` flag is explicitly designed (see `reply-payload.ts` comment) for "internal OpenClaw notices generated after a runtime/provider failure" and similar system-meta replies where the channel adapter must still be invoked. Command-handler terminal replies (`/compact`, `/status`, etc.) are user-initiated explicit feedback — they belong in the same category. Other system notices (model-fallback warning, embedded-runner error payloads) already use the same marker; this PR aligns command replies with that existing contract instead of inventing a new escape.
- **Risk**: removing the callback-gate on `notifyUser` could cause double-notification when an internal callback also writes to the user channel.
- **Mitigation**: `onCompactionStart` / `onCompactionEnd` are documented internal observer callbacks (Control UI / system state); they do not write to user channels. The user-channel-writing audience is `hookMessages` (plugin hook authors), and that branch still suppresses the default notice via the unchanged `hookMessages.length > 0` gate. The existing `delivers compaction hook messages without duplicating notifyUser notices` case verifies that suppression stays intact.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Sessions / storage

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #87107
